content: how do I view the conflict resolution in linux-next? is it just looking at the diff of the merge commit?
I think just doing git show on the merge commit works.

hmm for today's next that gives me:

commit 570b733c9f574955143a7e95ac2c83c59514b487
Merge: a392302de7d5 2009a2d56969
Author: Stephen Rothwell <sfr@canb.auug.org.au>
Date:   Fri Jul 11 18:03:42 2025 +1000

    Merge branch 'rust-next' of https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux.git

    # Conflicts:
    #       rust/kernel/devres.rs
    #       rust/kernel/pci.rs
    #       rust/kernel/platform.rs
    #       rust/kernel/sync/poll.rs

diff --cc rust/kernel/devres.rs
index f43de3d77d61,d0e6c6e162c2..30d26a34816f
--- a/rust/kernel/devres.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/devres.rs
@@@ -49,10 -44,10 +49,10 @@@ struct Inner<T: Send>
  /// [`Devres`] users should make sure to simply free the corresponding backing resource in `T`'s
  /// [`Drop`] implementation.
  ///
- /// # Example
+ /// # Examples
  ///
  /// ```no_run
 -/// # use kernel::{bindings, c_str, device::{Bound, Device}, devres::Devres, io::{Io, IoRaw}};
 +/// # use kernel::{bindings, device::{Bound, Device}, devres::Devres, io::{Io, IoRaw}};
  /// # use core::ops::Deref;
  ///
  /// // See also [`pci::Bar`] for a real example.
diff --cc rust/kernel/platform.rs
index ced43367d900,0a6a6be732b2..63ef8c2ba103
--- a/rust/kernel/platform.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/platform.rs
@@@ -129,10 -122,10 +129,10 @@@ macro_rules! module_platform_driver
  ///
  /// Drivers must implement this trait in order to get a platform driver registered.
  ///
- /// # Example
+ /// # Examples
  ///
  ///```
 -/// # use kernel::{bindings, c_str, device::Core, of, platform};
 +/// # use kernel::{acpi, bindings, c_str, device::Core, of, platform};
  ///
  /// struct MyDriver;
  ///

and I guess that's just picking all changes to the two files with actual conflicts, since the others seem to be auto-resolved?
I usually just create a merge point (then I would resolve the conflict at the merge point) and push that merge point it out when sending PR.
I don't understand
Let me find an example ;-)
https://lore.kernel.org/rcu/Z99wJFZC1XlvA4vC@Mac.home/
I.e. you don't need to apply the changes usually
I just wanted to know how to view the resolutions in linux-next
Oops, I mis-read your message.
Did you get an email from Stephen?
no, I just wanted to learn a bit before it happens :)

Benno Lossin said:

and I guess that's just picking all changes to the two files with actual conflicts, since the others seem to be auto-resolved?

I think usually you want to avoid picking back conflict resolution, it may break your branch, for example, rust-next doesn't have kernel::acpi yet. So if you apply the conflict resolution to rust-next then you will break `rust-next.
I asked for the case when pin-init-next would have a conflict with eg rust-next. I would then like to see how it was resolved by Stephen in order to check if it is correct

There appears no conflict when merging pin-init-next:

commit 54a8f372e81b0b463406f6ad174332f39139c785
Merge: 010a5da5adbf fc3870dc5cad
Author: Stephen Rothwell <sfr@canb.auug.org.au>
Date:   Fri Jul 11 18:03:45 2025 +1000

    Merge branch 'pin-init-next' of https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux.git
(I used git show as Alice said)

Take a look at the options on how to show diffs for merges: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-show#Documentation/git-show.txt---diff-mergesformat

In particular, the --remerge-diff one shows how Stephen resolved the conflict today by showing you how he removed the markers and one of the sides entirely (since the line modified in rust-next is not needed anymore).
Thanks! I never know that before.
I was being lazy: just wait for emails from Stephen and trust that guy named "Linus"
You're welcome!

Miguel Ojeda said:

Take a look at the options on how to show diffs for merges: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-show#Documentation/git-show.txt---diff-mergesformat

In particular, the --remerge-diff one shows how Stephen resolved the conflict today by showing you how he removed the markers and one of the sides entirely (since the line modified in rust-next is not needed anymore).

ahh thanks a lot, this is exactly what I was looking for!
is there a way to make that the default in git show? I found log.diffMerges, but that only sets the "default", so I have to pass --diff-merges=on regardless...

You're welcome!

If the config doesn't allow for it, then I would normally put it in my shorthand alias.
